title: Qwen3 与 DeepSeek7B 句子上下文词性因果实验

noun.sentence: The [dolphin] swam beside the research boat at dawn.
The [sailor] checked the ropes before the storm arrived.
The [bartender] polished the glasses after closing time.
The red [kayak] drifted toward the quiet shore.
The vast [glacier] cracked loudly in the afternoon sun.
The white [yacht] disappeared beyond the harbor wall.
The ripe [pineapple] rested on the kitchen counter.
The young [shark] circled the reef before sunset.
The [lawyer] reviewed the contract before the meeting.
The [whale] surfaced near the ice and sprayed water.
The hot [burger] cooled slowly on the plate.
The old [canoe] scraped against the rocks in the stream.

adjective.sentence: The [yellow] lantern glowed through the fog.
The [bright] screen lit the entire room.
The [dark] tunnel echoed with distant steps.
The [purple] scarf matched her winter coat.
The [green] field stretched toward the hills.
A [thick] blanket covered the wooden bench.
The [small] drawer held the missing key.
The [cold] wind pushed against the windows.
A [huge] shadow moved across the wall.
The [warm] bread filled the house with a sweet smell.
The [dirty] boots stood near the door.
A [thin] line of smoke rose above the fire.

verb.sentence: They will [create] a new map before lunch.
Please [write] the answer on the top line.
We should [take] the earlier train tomorrow.
Can you [make] a copy of this page?
Please [give] the key to the guard.
Children [learn] patterns faster than adults in this game.
You must [choose] one route before we leave.
The teacher will [explain] the rule once more.
Could you [bring] the notebook to my desk?
Try to [remember] the code after reading it.
Engineers [solve] the failure before sunrise.
Students [apply] the formula to each case.

adverb.sentence: She [quickly] solved the final puzzle.
The lights [usually] turn on at sunset.
The lake is [mostly] calm in early spring.
They [rarely] cancel the morning flight.
We [often] meet near the library steps.
He will [probably] arrive after the rain stops.
The runner [nearly] missed the narrow gate.
The road was [partly] blocked by snow.
The battery is [almost] empty now.
She [really] likes the old piano.
The door [slowly] opened without a sound.
The witness [clearly] saw the license number.

pronoun.sentence: [They] opened the gate after the bell rang.
[We] finished the report before dinner.
[You] left the umbrella by the stairs.
[He] carried the boxes into the hallway.
[She] repaired the clock with steady hands.
[It] rolled under the table during the game.
[This] belongs on the top shelf.
[That] explains the sudden change in tone.
[These] need more time in the oven.
[Who] called the office so late at night?
[Which] fits the lock on the blue cabinet?
[Whose] was found near the fountain?

preposition.sentence: The cat slept [under] the table all morning.
She arrived [with] her younger brother at noon.
The cabin stood [between] two dark pine trees.
The crowd remained quiet [during] the ceremony.
We walked home [after] the concert ended.
Sunlight passed [through] the narrow window.
The fox slipped [into] the empty barn.
Check the oil [before] the long drive begins.
The answer was hidden [within] the final paragraph.
They argued [about] the budget for an hour.
A cold draft came [from] the broken vent.
He left [without] his notebook by mistake.
